originalTitle: 'We want a voice in our land' - the people evicted to build Nigeria's capital
title: A Home for Everyone: The Story of Abuja
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends all across Africa! I'm your friendly news reporter, here to share a very important story with you today. It's about a place called Nigeria, a super cool country right here in Africa!

A Special City Called Abuja

Every country has a special city where its government works. This city is called the capital city. Think of it like the "headquarters" of a country! For a long time, Nigeria's capital was a busy city called Lagos. Lagos was full of people, cars, and markets – buzzing with life! But Lagos became very, very big and crowded. So, the leaders of Nigeria decided to build a brand new capital city. They picked a place right in the middle of the country. This new special city is called Abuja! It's a beautiful city now, with wide roads and important buildings. It has big buildings where important decisions are made for the whole country.

Who Lived There Before?

Now, here's the important part of our story. When they decided to build Abuja, there were already people living in that land. These people had their homes there. They had their farms there, where they grew food like yams and maize. They had their families and their friends all around them. This land was their home, their whole world. Imagine someone coming to your house and saying, "We need this land to build something new." That's what happened to these people. They were asked to leave their homes. This is what we call being "evicted." It means being told to move out of your home, even if you don't want to. It can be a very sad thing for families. Did you know? Nigeria is the most populated country in Africa! That means lots and lots of people live there.

Promises Made

The government understood that it was hard for these people to leave their homes. So, they made some very important promises. A promise is like saying, "I will definitely do this for you." The government promised to help these families. They promised to give them new homes to live in. They promised to give them new land where they could farm again. And they promised to give them some money to help them start their new lives. These promises were very important to the people. They felt sad to leave their homes, but they hoped for a better future with the government's help. They believed in the promises.

Promises Broken

But sadly, my friends, something went wrong. Many, many years have passed since these people moved out of their homes. Some of them moved out in the 1980s! That's a very, very long time ago – even before your parents were born! These people are now saying that the government did not keep all of its promises. They are saying that they did not get the new homes they were promised. They did not get the new land to farm. And some did not get the money they needed to start fresh. They feel like they were forgotten. Imagine if your friend promised to share their toy with you, but then they never did. How would that make you feel? You would feel sad, right? Maybe a little bit confused or even angry. That's how these people feel. They feel left out and forgotten.

We Want a Voice!

The people who were evicted have a very strong message. They say, "We want a voice in our land!" What does it mean to have a voice? It means they want to be heard. They want to be able to tell their story. They want to remind the government about the promises that were made many years ago. They want the government to remember them. They want the government to help them now, just like they promised a long time ago. They want to be treated fairly and with kindness. They want a chance to live well, just like everyone else in Nigeria. They just want what was promised to them. Did you know? Abuja officially became Nigeria's capital city in 1991! That's when it really started to grow big and important.

Why Is This Important?

This story is important because it teaches us about keeping promises. When we make a promise, it's very important to try our best to keep it. Keeping promises makes people trust us. It makes people feel safe and happy. When promises are broken, people can feel hurt and sad for a very long time. It also teaches us about being fair. Everyone deserves a safe home and a chance to live happily. These people gave up their homes so that Nigeria could have a beautiful new capital city. Now, they hope that the government will remember them and help them. They are asking for their fair share.

What Can We Learn?

From this story, we can all learn to be kind to each other. We can learn to listen when people are sad or need help. And we can learn that if we make a promise, we should always try our very best to keep it. Let's hope that the people who were evicted will get the help they need. Let's hope their voices are heard, and the promises are finally kept. Because everyone deserves a home and a fair chance in life! It's important for people to be happy and have a safe place to live. Thank you for listening, my wonderful African friends! See you next time with more important news!
summary: People who moved from their homes to build Nigeria's capital city, Abuja, say the government did not keep its promises to help them get new homes and land.
whyMatters: This story is important because it shows why keeping promises is so important and why everyone deserves a home and to be treated fairly.

title: Burkina Faso must 'forget' about democracy, military leader says
content: Capt Ibrahim Traoré, who seized power in 2023, says democratic rule would not work for his country.

originalTitle: Iran war: What is happening on day 36 of US-Israeli attacks?
title: Big Planes, Big Sky, and a Special Search!
content:
Hello everyone! It's me, your friendly news reporter for Africa! I'm here to share some news with you from far, far away. Sometimes, big things happen in the world, and it's good to learn about them, even if they are a bit serious. Don't worry, we'll talk about it in a simple way, just for you!

A Big Story From Far Away

Imagine our big, beautiful world with many, many countries, just like we have many towns in our country. Two of these countries are called America and Iran. They are very, very far from Africa, across big oceans. Sometimes, countries don't always agree on things, a bit like when friends might have a disagreement in the playground. When countries have big disagreements, things can sometimes get a bit tricky. Now, for a little while, there have been some serious problems between America and Iran. It's like a big problem that many grown-ups are trying to solve. In this big story, something happened recently that we need to tell you about.

What Are These Special Planes?

America has very special planes. These are not the planes you fly on for holidays, but even bigger and faster! They are sometimes called 'warplanes' because they are used for special jobs, like protecting a country or flying in big training exercises. Think of them like very strong, very fast birds made of metal, with pilots who are very brave and skilled at flying. Did you know? The biggest plane ever built can carry hundreds of people and many cars! But these planes we are talking about are made for speed and special missions. So, two of these very special planes from America were flying. But something happened, and these two planes came down from the sky. It's like if a kite you were flying suddenly fell to the ground. This made many people sad and worried.

Who Said What Happened?

A country called Iran, which is one of the countries having a problem with America, said that they were involved when the planes came down. It means they told everyone, 'Yes, we had a part in this happening.' This is a very serious thing for countries to say. When something like this happens, it makes the problems between the countries even bigger. It's like when you're playing with friends, and someone breaks a toy; everyone wants to know what happened and why.

Looking For a Friend

When the planes came down, there were brave people flying them. These people are called pilots or crew members. They are very important because they know how to fly these big, fast machines. Everyone who flies a plane is a hero because it's a very difficult and special job! After the planes came down, people counted how many crew members were safe. But sadly, one person who was on one of those planes is now missing. We don't know where they are right now, and that makes many people feel very sad and worried. Imagine if your favourite teddy bear went missing; you'd want to look everywhere for it, wouldn't you? Did you know? Search and rescue teams are special people who train very hard to find lost people, even in difficult places like mountains or the sea. They use big boats, helicopters, and sometimes even special dogs to help them! Right now, many people are looking for this missing person. They are doing a very big search, hoping to find them safe. They are looking in the area where the planes came down. Everyone hopes they will be found soon.

Why This Is Important

When something like this happens, it's very important because it shows that the problem between America and Iran is still very big. When people are hurt or go missing, it makes everyone think seriously about how to make things better. We hear about these things on the news because it helps us understand what is happening in the world. It reminds us that people in different countries need to try their best to be kind to each other and solve problems by talking, not by fighting. Did you know? The United Nations is like a super big club where almost all the countries in the world are members. They try to help countries talk to each other so they can solve problems peacefully!

Hoping For Good News

We all hope that the missing person is found safe and sound very soon. And we also hope that the countries of America and Iran can find a way to stop having problems and start being friends again. It's always better when everyone gets along, isn't it? Remember, even when things are serious, there are always people working to make things better and safer for everyone. We will keep listening and hoping for good news from far away. Stay safe and kind, everyone!
summary: Two special planes from America fell from the sky, and Iran said they were involved, making a big search for a missing person who was on one of the planes.
whyMatters: This news is important because it shows that countries sometimes have big problems, and when special planes come down and people go missing, everyone hopes they will be found safe and that countries can learn to be friends and talk instead of having disagreements.

originalTitle: European Parliament’s Rima Hassan: I’m a target of ‘political harassment’
title: A Special Lady Feels Unfairly Treated! Let's Learn About Speaking Up!
content:
Hello, my super amazing friends all over Africa! I'm your friendly news reporter, and I have a story for you today. It's about a kind lady named Rima Hassan. She is a very important person. She works in a big, big place far away in Europe. This place is called the European Parliament. Can you say that? Euro-pe-an Par-lia-ment! It’s like a giant school council for many countries! These grown-ups meet there to make good rules for everyone. They want to make the world a better place.

Meet Rima Hassan!

Our story starts with Rima Hassan. She is a brave woman. She is a special member of that big European Parliament. This means she is one of the grown-ups who helps make rules. She speaks up for people. She shares her ideas to help everyone live well. She works very hard every day. She wants to make sure people are treated fairly. She wants everyone to have a good life. Rima is like a super helper for many, many people. She has important work to do. She talks about what is right and what is wrong. She listens to different ideas. She helps decide what new rules can help a lot of people. It is a big job! She helps make the world a more loving and fair place for children and grown-ups. Did you know? Africa has its own big meetings too! Like the African Union, where leaders from African countries meet to help our continent grow strong and happy!

What is the European Parliament?

Imagine your school. You have a headteacher, right? And maybe some teachers who help make rules for your classroom. Well, the European Parliament is a bit like that, but for many countries! It’s a very big building where people from many different countries in Europe come together. They talk about big ideas. They talk about things like how to keep everyone safe. They talk about how to help people get good jobs. They talk about how to protect our Earth. They vote on new rules. These rules help many, many people live better lives. Rima Hassan is one of these important people who helps make those rules. She travels to this big building to do her work. It is a place where many voices are heard. It is a place for big, important talks. It is a place for making choices that affect millions of people, just like the choices we make in our homes and schools affect our families and friends.

Rima Feels Unfairly Treated

Now, for the sad part of our story. Rima Hassan says something is not right. She says she is facing “genuine judicial and political harassment”. Wow, those are big words, right? Let's break them down. When someone says they are facing ‘harassment’, it means that other people are making it very hard for them. It means people might be trying to stop them from doing their good work. It means they might be trying to make her feel bad. Imagine if someone kept picking on you at school. Or someone kept trying to stop you from playing your favourite game, even when you hadn't done anything wrong. That would feel really unfair, wouldn't it? That's a little bit like what harassment is. It’s when someone treats you badly or unfairly, over and over again. It can make you feel sad or scared. No one likes to feel like that. Rima is feeling this kind of unfairness, and it makes her work harder than it should be. Did you know? Speaking up when you see something unfair happening is called being 'courageous'! It means you are brave!

What is ‘Judicial and Political Harassment’?

Let's try to understand those other big words: 'judicial' and 'political'. 'Judicial' is about rules and laws. It’s about being fair and making sure everyone follows the rules. Like when your teacher makes rules for the classroom, and everyone needs to follow them so that school is a happy place. If someone tries to use these rules against Rima, not in a fair way, that can be judicial harassment. It means people might be trying to get her into trouble with the rules, even when she hasn't done anything wrong, or not in a truly fair way. They might be trying to make it seem like she broke a rule when she didn't. This can be very confusing and upsetting for anyone. 'Political' is about ideas. It's about having different thoughts on how to make things better for everyone. Like when you and your friend have different ideas about what game to play, but you both want to have fun. In the European Parliament, people have different 'political ideas' about how to solve problems. Rima has her own ideas. She thinks they are good ideas to help people. But some people might not like her ideas. When they try to stop her from doing her job because of her ideas, that is 'political harassment'. It means they are trying to silence her or make her job impossible because they don't agree with her, even if she is trying her best to help. This kind of harassment can make it very hard for Rima to speak up for people and make good changes.

Why is This Important for All of Us?

This is important, my friends, because everyone, everywhere, deserves to be treated fairly. Whether you are a child in school, a grown-up working, or a brave lady like Rima Hassan in a big Parliament, fairness matters. When someone feels unfairly treated, it means something is wrong. We all want to live in a world where people can speak up. We want a world where people can share their good ideas without being made to feel bad or stopped unfairly. Rima is speaking up for herself. She is telling everyone that this is not fair. She is asking for help. She wants the unfairness to stop. It reminds us all that we should always try to be kind and fair to others. We should listen to each other, even if we have different ideas. And if someone is being treated badly, we should try to help them. Always remember, speaking up for what is right is very important. Did you know? In many African cultures, 'Ubuntu' means 'I am because we are', teaching us that we are all connected and should care for each other! So, my dear friends, let's wish Rima Hassan strength. Let's hope that the unfairness stops very soon. Let's remember to be kind, fair, and brave like her every day. We can all make a difference in our own small way. You can make a difference by being a good friend and standing up for what is right in your school and home. Goodbye for now, and keep shining bright!
summary: Rima Hassan, an important lady in the big European Parliament, says some people are treating her unfairly and making her job very hard because of her ideas and how she helps people.
whyMatters: It matters because everyone deserves to be treated fairly, and people like Rima Hassan should be able to do their important work and speak up without being picked on or stopped unfairly.

originalTitle: Rescue team in Iran face 'harrowing and dangerous' search for US crew member
title: Rescue team in Iran face 'harrowing and dangerous' search for US crew member
content: A former US marine tells the BBC the priority of any recovery team would be to look for signs of life.

originalTitle: Artemis II crew take 'spectacular' image of Earth
title: Wow! Astronauts Take a Super Picture of Earth from Space!
content:
Hello, amazing explorers and future scientists! Your friendly kid-friendly reporter from Africa is here with some exciting news from way, way up high! Imagine looking at our beautiful home, Planet Earth, from very, very far away. Guess what? Some brave people are doing just that right now!

A Super Picture from Far, Far Away!

Our Earth is a big, beautiful ball. It's our home! We live on it, play on it, and learn on it. But what does it look like from high up in space? Well, some amazing astronauts are flying towards the Moon! They are on a special trip. And guess what they did? They took a picture! This picture is not just any picture. It's a picture of our own Planet Earth! It was taken from their super speedy spaceship. A very brave astronaut named Reid Wiseman took the picture. He is the boss of the spaceship. He looked out of the window and saw something truly amazing. He saw our Earth! From far, far away in space, our Earth looked like a super beautiful blue marble. It had white swirly clouds all around it. How cool is that? It must have felt so special to see Earth from there. Like looking back at your own house from very, very far away. He made sure to snap a picture for all of us to see!

Meet the Brave Space Explorers!

Who are these brave people flying to the Moon? They are called astronauts! They are like super explorers, but instead of exploring new lands here, they explore space! This group of astronauts is part of something called Artemis II. They are a team of four very special people. They are going on a big trip around the Moon. Their job is to fly in their special spaceship, get close to the Moon, and then come back home safely. They will learn so many new things for all of us. They are learning how to fly to the Moon safely. This is a very big step for future trips. Maybe one day, they will build houses on the Moon! Did you know? Astronauts train for many, many years! They learn about science, flying, and how to live in space. It's like going to a very special school for space adventurers! They wear special suits when they are outside their spaceship. These suits keep them safe and warm in the cold, empty space.

What is the Orion Spaceship?

How do these astronauts travel so far? They travel in a very special vehicle! It's called the Orion capsule. Think of it like a super-duper car, but for space! The Orion capsule is their home in space. It's where they eat, sleep, and work. It's like a tiny house flying through the stars. This spaceship is made to go very, very fast. It can travel all the way to the Moon and back. It protects the astronauts from the dangers of space, like tiny rocks and very cold temperatures. It has big windows so the astronauts can look out. That's how Reid Wiseman saw our Earth and took that amazing photo! Did you know? The Orion capsule is very strong! It is built to keep the astronauts safe even when it gets very hot or very cold in space. It's like a superhero shield! The capsule also has special computers that help the astronauts know where they are going. It’s like a super smart map for space travel!

Our Amazing Home: Planet Earth!

When Reid Wiseman looked out, he saw our Earth. Our Earth is a very special planet. It's the only place we know where people and animals can live! From space, Earth looks like a big blue marble. The blue parts are our big oceans and seas. The green and brown parts are the land, where we have countries and forests. And the white swirls? Those are clouds! They float high in the sky and bring us rain, which helps our plants grow. It's so important to take care of our Earth. It's our only home! Seeing it from space makes us remember how special and beautiful it is. Did you know? Earth is always spinning! That's why we have day and night. When your part of Earth faces the sun, it's day. When it faces away, it's night! Earth also has a special blanket of air around it. This blanket is called the atmosphere, and it helps us breathe and keeps us safe from too much sun.

Why Go to the Moon?

Why are these brave astronauts going all the way to the Moon? Is it just for a holiday? No! They are going to learn many new things. The Moon is Earth's closest neighbor in space. It's a big, rocky ball that shines brightly in our night sky. Scientists want to learn more about the Moon. They want to know how it was made. They want to know if people can live there one day. This trip is like a big test. It helps them learn how to fly to the Moon safely. And maybe, one day, people will go even further! Did you know? The Moon does not make its own light. It shines because the Sun's light bounces off it! It's like a giant mirror in the sky. The Moon also has no air. So, astronauts must wear their special suits all the time if they step out onto the Moon's surface!

Dreams of Space!

Seeing this picture of Earth from space, and hearing about astronauts flying to the Moon, can make us all dream big! Maybe one day, you could be an astronaut! Or maybe you could be a scientist who helps design spaceships. Or maybe you could be an artist who draws pictures of space! The world needs curious kids like you. Look up at the sky at night. See the stars twinkle. Maybe you'll see the Moon and think about the brave astronauts on their way! Learning about space helps us understand our place in the universe. It shows us how important our Earth is and how much there is to explore. Keep asking questions and keep learning! Who knows what amazing things you will discover!
summary: Astronaut Reid Wiseman on the Artemis II mission took a beautiful picture of our Earth from his spaceship while flying towards the Moon, showing us our home planet from far, far away.
whyMatters: This picture helps us remember how special our Earth is and shows us that brave people are exploring space to learn new things for everyone, inspiring us all to dream big about science and adventure.
